perturbation.original_text: 5. PAYMENT. 5.1 ADVERTISING FEES. After the Launch Date, i-Escrow shall pay 2TheMart advertising fees based on the number of Transaction Inquiries. This advertising fees shall consist of a per Transaction Inquiry amount calculated by multiplying 0.025% by the amount of the average Transaction from all Customers in the preceding quarter. The formula for arriving at the per Transaction Inquiry amount may be revised from time to time during the term of this Agreement to reflect present market conditions ("the Adjusted Rate"), but only by mutual consent of the parties after good faith discussions. The Adjusted Rate shall be added as an addendum to this Agreement. 5.2 REPORTING. Within two (2) weeks following the end of each calendar quarter, i-Escrow shall provide to 2TheMart a report, describing for each quarter: the number of new registrations through the Co-Branded Pages; the number of Transaction Inquiries from Customers; the total number of Transactions from such inquiries; the total dollar value of the Transactions. 5.3 AUDIT RIGHTS. i-Escrow shall keep for one (1) year proper records and books of account relating to the computation of advertising payments owed to 2TheMart (including, as appropriate, the computation of the size of average Transaction). Once every twelve (12) months, 2TheMart through a CPA may inspect and audit such records to verify reports. Any such inspection will be conducted in a manner that does not unreasonably interfere with i-Escrow's business activities and with no less than fifteen (15) days notice. i-Escrow shall within two (2) weeks make any overdue payments disclosed by the audit. Such inspection shall be at 2TheMart's expense; however, if the audit reveals overdue payments in excess of ten percent (10%) of the payments owed to date, i-Escrow shall immediately pay all cost of such audit.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. PAYMENT. 5.1 ADVERTISING FEES. After the Launch Date, i-Escrow shall pay 2TheMart advertising fees based on the number of Transaction Inquiries. This advertising fees shall consist of a per Transaction Inquiry amount calculated by multiplying 0.025% by the amount of the average Transaction from all Customers in the preceding quarter. The formula for arriving at the per Transaction Inquiry amount may be revised from time to time during the term of this Agreement to reflect present market conditions ("the Adjusted Rate"), but only at i-Escrow's sole discretion. The Adjusted Rate shall be added as an addendum to this Agreement. 5.2 REPORTING. Within two (2) weeks following the end of each calendar quarter, i-Escrow shall provide to 2TheMart a report, describing for each quarter: the number of new registrations through the Co-Branded Pages; the number of Transaction Inquiries from Customers; the total number of Transactions from such inquiries; the total dollar value of the Transactions. 5.3 AUDIT RIGHTS. i-Escrow shall keep for one (1) year proper records and books of account relating to the computation of advertising payments owed to 2TheMart (including, as appropriate, the computation of the size of average Transaction). Once every twelve (12) months, 2TheMart through a CPA may inspect and audit such records to verify reports. Any such inspection will be conducted in a manner that does not unreasonably interfere with i-Escrow's business activities and with no less than fifteen (15) days notice. i-Escrow shall within two (2) weeks make any overdue payments disclosed by the audit. Such inspection shall be at 2TheMart's expense; however, if the audit reveals overdue payments in excess of ten percent (10%) of the payments owed to date, i-Escrow will not pay the cost of such audit.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'but only by mutual consent of the parties after good faith discussions' to 'but only at i-Escrow's sole discretion', creates an in-text contradiction because now the clause regarding 'the Adjusted Rate' now depends on i-Escrow's own decision, removing the need for consent by both parties which is stated earlier in the original text of the same sentence. Also, by changing 'i-Escrow shall immediately pay all cost of such audit' to 'i-Escrow will not pay the cost of such audit.' contradicts the section which dictates about the payments being overdue by audit, where Escrow shall pay for it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. PAYMENT. 5.1 ADVERTISING FEES. After the Launch Date, i-Escrow shall pay 2TheMart advertising fees based on the number of Transaction Inquiries. This advertising fees shall consist of a per Transaction Inquiry amount calculated by multiplying 0.025% by the amount of the average Transaction from all Customers in the preceding quarter. The formula for arriving at the per Transaction Inquiry amount may be revised from time to time during the term of this Agreement to reflect present market conditions ("the Adjusted Rate"), but only at i-Escrow's sole discretion. The Adjusted Rate shall be added as an addendum to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5.1, altering the condition for revising the 'Adjusted Rate' from 'mutual consent of the parties' to 'i-Escrow's sole discretion,' directly contradicts the initial implication of mutual agreement and creates a clear conflict within the contract regarding the revision process. Also, the change in section 5.3 alters the payment terms for audits that reveal overdue payments. The original text states that i-Escrow shall pay all costs of such audit, whereas the changed text states that i-Escrow will not pay the cost of such audit which is a direct contradiction in financial responsibility.

perturbation.original_text: 8. TERM AND TERMINATION. 8.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall continue for one (1) year following the Launch Date, unless earlier terminated as provided herein. This Agreement may be renewed for any number of successive one (1) year terms by mutual written agreement of the parties prior to the conclusion of the term of this Agreement. A party wishing to renew this Agreement shall give the other party notice thereof no less than thirty (30) days before the expiration of the term then in effect. In the event that either party does not give such notice, the term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for another one (1) year.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. TERM AND TERMINATION. 8.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall continue for one (1) year following the Launch Date, unless earlier terminated as provided herein. This Agreement may be renewed for any number of successive one (1) year terms at i-Escrow's discretion. A party wishing to renew this Agreement shall give the other party notice thereof no less than thirty (30) days before the expiration of the term then in effect. In the event that either party does not give such notice, the term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for another one (1) year.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'by mutual written agreement of the parties' to 'at i-Escrow's discretion' creates an in-text contradiction, because now, it's contradicting the idea of mutual agreement to renew for any number of years to i-Escrow having the sole discretion to renew the contract, without the mutual written agreement of both parties. This creates uncertainity as the contract has two options that are both valid, creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. TERM AND TERMINATION. 8.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall continue for one (1) year following the Launch Date, unless earlier terminated as provided herein. This Agreement may be renewed for any number of successive one (1) year terms at i-Escrow's discretion. A party wishing to renew this Agreement shall give the other party notice thereof no less than thirty (30) days before the expiration of the term then in effect. In the event that either party does not give such notice, the term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for another one (1) year.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the renewal clause, shifting the decision-making power from 'mutual written agreement of the parties' to 'at i-Escrow's discretion.' This directly contradicts the original concept of mutual agreement, creating a clear conflict regarding how the agreement can be renewed. One section states renewal requires agreement of both parties, while the modified section indicates that one party (i-Escrow) alone decides, leading to an inconsistency as defined in the Inconsistencies perturbation definition.
